books.title: Harry Potter and the Philosopher's Stone
books.author: J.K. Rowling
books.description: The story of a young boy who discovers on his 11th birthday that he is a wizard. Harry Potter enters Hogwarts School of Witchcraft and Wizardry and begins his journey into the magical world, uncovering the truth about his parents and the dark wizard Voldemort.

books.title: Harry Potter and the Chamber of Secrets
books.author: J.K. Rowling
books.description: Harry Potter's second year at Hogwarts brings new terrors. A mysterious creature is attacking students, and Harry keeps hearing a strange voice inside the walls. The Chamber of Secrets has been opened — and the heir of Slytherin has returned.

books.title: Harry Potter and the Prisoner of Azkaban
books.author: J.K. Rowling
books.description: In his third year, Harry learns that a dangerous prisoner named Sirius Black has escaped from Azkaban and is believed to be coming for him. This installment introduces time travel, new magical creatures, and deeper mysteries about Harry's past.

books.author: George Orwell
books.description: A dystopian novel set in a totalitarian society ruled by Big Brother. Winston Smith struggles against oppression in a world of constant surveillance, propaganda, and the erasure of truth and individuality.

books.title: The Pragmatic Programmer
books.author: Andrew Hunt & David Thomas
books.description: A timeless guide for software developers covering best practices, philosophies, and tips to become a more effective and pragmatic programmer. Essential reading for both junior and senior developers.

books.title: Sapiens: A Brief History of Humankind
books.author: Yuval Noah Harari
books.description: A sweeping history of humanity from the Stone Age to the modern era. Harari explores how Homo sapiens came to dominate Earth through cognitive revolution, agricultural revolution, and scientific revolution.

books.title: To Kill a Mockingbird
books.author: Harper Lee
books.description: A Pulitzer Prize-winning novel set in the American South during the 1930s. Seen through the eyes of young Scout Finch, it explores themes of racial injustice, moral growth, and compassion.

books.title: Clean Code
books.author: Robert C. Martin
books.description: A guide to writing clean, maintainable code. Robert C. Martin presents a paradigm for software craftsmanship, covering principles, patterns, and practices of writing clean code with real-world case studies.

books.title: The Selfish Gene
books.author: Richard Dawkins
books.description: A groundbreaking work in evolutionary biology that argues genes are the fundamental unit of natural selection. Dawkins introduces the concept of the meme and reshapes how we think about life and evolution.

books.title: The Alchemist
books.author: Paulo Coelho
books.description: A philosophical novel about a young Andalusian shepherd named Santiago who embarks on a journey to find a worldly treasure. Along the way, he discovers the deeper meaning of life and his personal legend.

books.title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
books.author: Martin Kleppmann
books.description: A comprehensive guide for engineers building data systems. Covers the principles behind reliable, scalable, and maintainable systems — from databases and distributed systems to data encoding and stream processing.

books.title: A Brief History of Time
books.author: Stephen Hawking
books.description: An iconic exploration of the universe from the Big Bang to black holes. Stephen Hawking brings complex cosmological concepts to a general audience, questioning the nature of time, space, and the cosmos.

books.title: The Bermuda Triangle
books.author: Charles Berlitz
books.description: The definitive book on one of the world's greatest mysteries. Charles Berlitz investigates the strange disappearances of ships and aircraft in the Bermuda Triangle region, exploring supernatural theories, underwater civilizations, and unexplained phenomena that have baffled scientists for decades.

books.title: The Philadelphia Experiment
books.author: Charles Berlitz & William Moore
books.description: The shocking true story of a secret US Navy experiment in 1943 that allegedly made a warship invisible and teleported it from Philadelphia to Norfolk. A deep dive into one of the most controversial and unexplained events in military history.

books.title: Chariots of the Gods
books.author: Erich von Däniken
books.description: A groundbreaking and controversial work that proposes ancient aliens visited Earth thousands of years ago and influenced early human civilizations. Von Däniken examines ancient monuments, cave paintings, and myths as evidence of extraterrestrial contact.

books.title: The Da Vinci Code
books.author: Dan Brown
books.description: A Harvard symbologist is drawn into a deadly conspiracy after a murder inside the Louvre reveals a trail of clues hidden in the works of Leonardo da Vinci. A race through secret history, ancient codes, and religious mystery that will change everything you thought you knew.

books.title: Inferno
books.author: Dan Brown
books.description: Robert Langdon wakes up in a Florence hospital with no memory of the last 36 hours. Thrust into a world of Dante-inspired symbols and a terrifying biological threat, he must race across Europe to prevent a global catastrophe.

books.title: The Immortality Key
books.author: Brian C. Muraresku
books.description: A groundbreaking investigation into the secret history of religion and the role of psychedelic ritual in ancient Greece and early Christianity. A 12-year investigation combining archaeology, linguistics, and pharmacology to uncover one of history's greatest secrets.

books.title: JavaScript: The Good Parts
books.author: Douglas Crockford
books.description: An in-depth look at the parts of JavaScript that make it an outstanding language, focusing on the best practices to write reliable, maintainable, and efficient code. A must-read for anyone who wants to truly master JavaScript.

books.title: Lost Technologies of Ancient Egypt
books.author: Christopher Dunn
books.description: A mechanical engineer examines the extraordinary precision of ancient Egyptian artifacts and monuments, arguing that the tools and techniques used to build them are far beyond what historians have credited to that civilization — pointing to a lost advanced technology.

features.title: Curated Collections
features.desc: Browse thousands of books organized by genre, mood, and topic — hand-picked to help you find your next great read.

features.title: Smart Search
features.desc: Find any book instantly by title, author, or category. Our search adapts as you type.

features.title: Save & Organize
features.desc: Bookmark your favorites, build reading lists, and track what you've already read.

features.title: Always Fresh
features.desc: New titles added regularly. The loop never stops — there's always something new waiting for you.

faqs.question: What is Book Loop?
faqs.answer: Book Loop is a free platform where you can discover, search, and save books across dozens of categories. Whether you love sci-fi, history, self-help, or fantasy — Book Loop helps you find your next great read without the overwhelm.

faqs.question: Is Book Loop completely free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, 100% free. There are no hidden charges, no premium tiers, and no subscription fees. Every feature — search, filter, bookmarking, and collections — is available to all users at no cost.

faqs.question: How do I search for a book by title or category?
faqs.answer: Use the search bar at the top of the page and start typing a book title — results appear instantly as you type. To browse by category, open the category drawer and select a genre like Story, Tech, or Science to filter the list.

faqs.question: Can I save or bookmark books for later?
faqs.answer: Absolutely. Hit the bookmark icon on any book card to save it to your personal reading list. Your saved books are stored and accessible whenever you come back, so you never lose track of something you wanted to read.

faqs.question: How often are new books added?
faqs.answer: We add new titles regularly across all categories. The loop never stops — there's always something fresh waiting for you. You can check the 'New Arrivals' section on the home page to see what's been added recently.
